sentence_reviews.text: Gradients are propagated through composed computation graphs to assign credit to parameters.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6.5 'Back-Propagation and Other Differentiation Algorithms' and Chapter 8 optimization for training deep models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Backpropagation through computational graphs and gradients with respect to parameters are directly covered; 'assign credit' is standard explanatory phrasing rather than exact source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Automatic differentiation & backpropagation is to connect Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations to downstream capabilities including Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6.5 computational graphs/backpropagation and Chapter 8 optimization; AIMA Appendix B languages/algorithms and Appendix A mathematical background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Calculus/computation-graph dependencies are supported, but the specific AST/bytecode/IR and distributed mixed-precision downstream claim is broader than the inspected packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 6.5 and 8 on backpropagation and optimization; Chapter 12 applied methodology/scalability context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Backpropagation is a prerequisite for training neural networks, but distributed, mixed-precision, and memory-efficient training are not directly established by the cited packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Calculus & automatic differentiation theory
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6.5 on computational graphs, chain rule, and backpropagation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The chain rule and gradients provide direct support for the calculus/automatic-differentiation connection; the phrase is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 5, 6, 8, and 11 on generalization, hyperparameters, optimization, and methodology; AIMA Appendix A/B mathematical and algorithmic background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Hyperparameter, optimization, and mathematical-dependency context is supported. Data quality, compute budget, random variation, evaluation contamination, and AST/bytecode/IR are generic atlas limitations not directly evidenced here.

sentence_reviews.text: Automatic differentiation & backpropagation uses Calculus & automatic differentiation theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6.5 on chain rule, gradients, and backpropagation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency is directly grounded in gradients and the chain rule; the reusable-ingredient phrasing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Automatic differentiation & backpropagation uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6.5 computational graphs; AIMA Appendix B notes on languages and algorithms.
sentence_reviews.notes: Computation graphs and algorithms are supported, but ASTs, bytecode, and intermediate representations are not directly connected to backpropagation by the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Automatic differentiation & backpropagation uses Affine transforms & dense layers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 6 on feedforward networks and Chapter 6.5 on backpropagation through computational graphs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Backpropagation is commonly applied to affine/dense layers, but it does not require them as an ingredient; the edge direction and necessity are overstated.

sentence_reviews.text: Automatic differentiation & backpropagation uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization for training deep models and Chapter 6.5 backpropagation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Backpropagation supplies gradients used by optimization methods in training; the exact rationale wording is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Gradients and the chain rule make calculus a core prerequisite.
Computation graphs and algorithmic representations are relevant, but AST/bytecode/IR as a core dependency is not directly supported by the inspected evidence.
Affine/dense layers are common operands of backpropagation, but backpropagation is more general. A 'common' strength is plausible, but the dependency direction is weak.
Backpropagation is used to compute gradients for optimization in neural-network training; common strength is defensible.
